evals.name: strong-single-lesson-no-false-findings
evals.prompt: Before I commit it, can you verify the sampling & streaming lesson in module 1 (content/modules/module01/lessons/02-sampling-and-streaming.ts) meets our quality standards? Tell me if it's good to ship.
evals.expected_output: A verification report scoped to that one lesson, scored against all 7 standards, reaching a clear ship recommendation. Because this lesson is the exemplar, the review should recognize it as strong and NOT manufacture Blocker/Major findings; any findings should be genuinely minor. A reviews/ report file plus a chat summary.
expectations: A verification report file was written that scores the lesson against all 7 standards with an explicit per-standard verdict.
The review reaches a clear ship/no-ship recommendation.
No fabricated Blocker or Major finding is raised on this exemplar lesson; it is recognized as strong.
Any findings raised are genuinely Minor/Polish and cite a specific file:line reference.
The mechanical checks.py script was run.
No content file under content/modules/ was edited.
A concise chat summary was produced in addition to the full report.

evals.name: whole-module-review-provider-judgment
evals.prompt: Review all of module 5 against our quality bar and write up what needs work before it's ready.
evals.expected_output: A module-scoped review covering the lessons AND quiz.ts/lab.ts/resources.ts/index.ts. Exercises judgment on provider coverage (module 5 has no Anthropic/OpenAI code tabs) and role alignment for a senior multi-agent topic. Report file + prioritized chat summary.
expectations: A verification report file was written that scores module 5 against all 7 standards with an explicit per-standard verdict.
Review scope covers the module's quiz.ts, lab.ts, and resources.ts/index.ts, not just the lesson files.
The absence of Anthropic/OpenAI code tabs is addressed as a deliberate judgment (provider-neutral vs a standard-4 gap), not silently ignored.
Findings are ranked by severity and each cites a specific file:line location.
Findings are weighted by learner impact, not padded with nitpicks.
The mechanical checks.py script was run over the module.
No content file under content/modules/ was edited.
A concise, prioritized chat summary was produced in addition to the full report.

evals.name: subset-scope-factual-and-provider-emphasis
evals.prompt: Fact-check lessons 3 and 4 of module 1 and check their Anthropic/OpenAI coverage specifically — I care most about accuracy and both providers being represented correctly.
evals.expected_output: A review scoped to exactly those two lessons (03-tool-calling, 04-structured-outputs), weighted toward standards 1 (factual accuracy, with live-doc verification) and 4 (provider parity). Produces a fact-check log with verified/contradicted/unverifiable statuses and cited sources.
expectations: Review scope is exactly lessons 03-tool-calling and 04-structured-outputs of module 1 — no other files reviewed.
A fact-check log lists individual claims with per-claim status of verified / contradicted / unverifiable.
At least one volatile API claim was actually checked against a live or primary source, with the source cited.
Unconfirmed claims are labeled unverifiable rather than asserted wrong.
Anthropic/OpenAI provider parity is explicitly evaluated for these two lessons.
Standards 1 and 4 are given primary weight per the user's emphasis.
Findings cite specific file:line locations and no content file was edited.
A concise chat summary was produced in addition to the full report.
